Title: Exogenous chalcone synthase expression in developing poplar xylem incorporates naringenin into lignins

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Naringenin/chalcone entry branch from phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step; branch point competing with monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product and diverts flux away from monolignol biosynthesis toward naringenin production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses one p-coumaroyl-CoA with three malonyl-CoA molecules to produce naringenin chalcone, which is subsequently isomerized by endogenous CHI to (2S)-naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-point control step for redirecting carbon from monolignol biosynthesis to flavonoid/naringenin production in engineered xylem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: developing/mature xylem; lignin polymer of secondary cell wall; soluble xylem extracts
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Valuable flavonoid compound; when incorporated into lignin may shorten polymer growth and improve biomass deconstruction/saccharification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ectopic xylem expression of MdCHS3 causes naringenin accumulation in poplar xylem, incorporation of naringenin into lignin, reduced total lignin, increased cell wall carbohydrate/cellulose content, and improved glucose/xylose release during saccharification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in developing and mature xylem; expression level in xylem positively correlated with naringenin accumulation; not detected in WT
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth for 16 weeks/4 months; saccharification tested with or without mild dilute-acid pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Endogenous chalcone isomerase (CHI) inferred for conversion of naringenin chalcone to (2S)-naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic xylem-specific overexpression in poplar; RT-qPCR; UPLC-DAD metabolite profiling; hydrolysis of glycosides; 2D 1H-13C HSQC NMR of extracts and enzyme lignin; Klason lignin analysis; thioacidolysis; carbohydrate analysis; limited saccharification assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Apple MdCHS3 expressed under the lignin-specific AtC4Hp promoter produced detectable naringenin in poplar xylem whereas WT had none. 2. NMR demonstrated that naringenin was present not only in soluble extracts but also incorporated into the lignin fraction of transgenic poplar. 3. Higher MdCHS3 expression reduced total lignin, increased some cell wall carbohydrates including alpha-cellulose, and improved glucose release during saccharification without major growth penalty.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering module for woody bioenergy crops to introduce lignin-compatible flavonoids, lower lignin recalcitrance, and improve saccharification. Can serve as a background for stacking additional flavonoid-pathway genes to produce other valuable lignin-incorporable compounds in xylem.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: One line was taller than WT, but overall biomass, stem diameter, and most height traits were not significantly altered. Unknown compounds and glycosides also accumulated; possible diversion of carbon from monolignol biosynthesis may alter cell wall composition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study emphasizes MdCHS3 as the core experimental gene; endogenous poplar CHS genes were measured mainly as background comparison and were expressed at much lower levels in WT xylem. The paper suggests endogenous CHI activity is sufficient, so no exogenous CHI was required. Naringenin likely incorporates into lignin via radical coupling and acts as a chain-terminating monomer.
